name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
ضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ ٱمْرَأَتَ نُوحٍ وَٱمْرَأَتَ لُوطٍ ۖ كَانَتَا تَحْتَ عَبْدَيْنِ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا صَـٰلِحَيْنِ فَخَانَتَاهُمَا فَلَمْ يُغْنِيَا عَنْهُمَا مِنَ ٱللَّهِ شَيْـًٔا وَقِيلَ ٱدْخُلَا ٱلنَّارَ مَعَ ٱلدَّٰخِلِينَ (١٠)
```

```english
Allah cites an example of the faithless: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two of our righteous servants, yet they betrayed them. So they did not avail them in any way against Allah, and it was said [to them], ‘Enter the Fire, along with those who enter [it].’ (10)
```

```arabic
وَضَرَبَ ٱللَّهُ مَثَلًا لِّلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ٱمْرَأَتَ فِرْعَوْنَ إِذْ قَالَتْ رَبِّ ٱبْنِ لِى عِندَكَ بَيْتًا فِى ٱلْجَنَّةِ وَنَجِّنِى مِن فِرْعَوْنَ وَعَمَلِهِۦ وَنَجِّنِى مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (١١)
```

```english
Allah cites an example of the faithful: the wife of Pharaoh, when she said, ‘My Lord! Build me a home near You in paradise, and deliver me from Pharaoh and his conduct, and deliver me from the wrongdoing lot.’ (11)
```

```arabic
وَمَرْيَمَ ٱبْنَتَ عِمْرَٰنَ ٱلَّتِىٓ أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَهَا فَنَفَخْنَا فِيهِ مِن رُّوحِنَا وَصَدَّقَتْ بِكَلِمَـٰتِ رَبِّهَا وَكُتُبِهِۦ وَكَانَتْ مِنَ ٱلْقَـٰنِتِينَ (١٢)
```

```english
And Mary, daughter of Imran, who guarded the chastity of her womb, so We breathed into it of Our spirit. She confirmed the words of her Lord and His Books, and she was one of the obedient. (12)
```

## (Commentary)

The noble verses contain two parables by which Allah, the Glorified, illustrates the condition of the disbelievers (الكفار) and the believers (المؤمنين). They demonstrate that the wretchedness and destruction of the disbelievers resulted solely from their betrayal of Allah and His Messenger and their disbelief (كفر); their connection by marriage to the honored Prophets did not benefit them in the least. Conversely, the felicity and success of the believers resulted solely from their sincere faith (إيمان) in Allah and His Messenger, their devout obedience (القنوت), and their righteous submission; their connection to the enemies of Allah by marriage did not harm them at all. Indeed, the sole criterion for honor in the sight of Allah is godwariness (التقوى) — *the state of being conscious of Allah's presence and guarding oneself against sin*.

He illustrates the situation first with the case of two women who were wives of two noble Prophets whom Allah, the Glorified, counted among His righteous servants (عبدين صالحين) — and what an immense honor that is! Yet, they betrayed them, and so they were commanded to enter the Fire along with those who enter. Their marriage to the two noble Prophets availed them nothing, and they perished among the doomed without the slightest distinction or honor.

Secondly, He illustrates it with the case of two other women. One was the wife of Pharaoh, whose status in disbelief (الكفر) in Allah reached the point where he proclaimed to the people, saying: "I am your lord, the most high." Yet, she believed in Allah and was sincere in her faith (الإيمان), so Allah delivered her and admitted her into Paradise; her marriage to the likes of Pharaoh did not harm her in the least. The second was Maryam, the daughter of ‘Imran, the truthful (الصديقة) and devoutly obedient (القانتة), whom Allah honored with His grace and into whom He breathed of His Spirit.

In these parables, there is a clear and severe allusion (تعريض) to the two wives of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), as they betrayed him by disclosing his secret, conspired against him, and caused him distress thereby—especially given the use of the terms "disbelief" (الكفر) and "betrayal" (الخيانة) and the mention of the command to enter the Fire.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا امْرَأَتَ نُوحٍ وَامْرَأَتَ لُوطٍ كانَتا تَحْتَ عَبْدَيْنِ مِنْ عِبادِنا صالِحَيْنِ فَخانَتاهُما** **"Allah draws an example for those who disbelieve: the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot. They were under two of Our righteous servants, yet they betrayed them"** (66:10). Al-Raghib stated: "Betrayal (الخيانة) and hypocrisy (النفاق) are one and the same, except that betrayal is used in consideration of covenants and trusts (الأمانة), while hypocrisy is used in consideration of religion (الدين); thereafter, they overlap. Thus, betrayal is the violation of truth by breaking a covenant in secret, and its opposite is trustworthiness (الأمانة). It is said: 'I betrayed so-and-so' or 'I betrayed the trust of so-and-so.'" [End of quote].

Regarding His saying: **لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا** **"for those who disbelieve,"** if it is linked to "the example" (المثل), the meaning would be: Allah has struck a parable that illustrates the condition of those who disbelieve, showing that connection to righteous servants does not benefit them. If it is linked to "draws" (ضرب), the meaning would be: Allah has set these two women and the end they met as an example for those who disbelieve, so that they may take heed and know that connection to the righteous among His servants will not benefit them, and that by their betrayal of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), they are inevitably among the people of the Fire.

His saying: **امْرَأَتَ نُوحٍ وَامْرَأَتَ لُوطٍ** **"the wife of Noah and the wife of Lot"** is the object of "draws" (ضرب). The meaning of their being "under them" (تحتهما) refers to their being their wives.

His saying: **فَلَمْ يُغْنِيا عَنْهُما مِنَ اللهِ شَيْئاً** **"so they did not avail them anything against Allah"** (66:10). The first dual pronoun refers to the two servants (the Prophets), and the second refers to the two women. The meaning is that the two women's marriage to the two righteous servants did not benefit them.

His saying: **وَقِيلَ ادْخُلَا النَّارَ مَعَ الدَّاخِلِينَ** **"and it was said, ‘Enter the Fire along with those who enter!’"** (66:10). That is, along with those entering it from their respective peoples, as is alluded to in His saying regarding the wife of Noah: **حَتَّى إِذا جاءَ أَمْرُنا وَفارَ التَّنُّورُ قُلْنَا احْمِلْ فِيها مِنْ كُلٍّ زَوْجَيْنِ اثْنَيْنِ وَأَهْلَكَ إِلَّا مَنْ سَبَقَ عَلَيْهِ الْقَوْلُ** **"until when Our edict came and the oven gushed [forth with water], We said, ‘Carry in it a pair of every kind [of animal], and your family, except those against whom the word has already gone forth’"** (11:40). And His saying regarding the wife of Lot: **فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ بِقِطْعٍ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ وَلا يَلْتَفِتْ مِنْكُمْ أَحَدٌ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَكَ إِنَّهُ مُصِيبُها ما أَصابَهُمْ** **"So set out with your family in a watch of the night, and let none of you look back, except your wife: indeed she will be struck by what strikes them"** (11:81). Alternatively, the meaning is "along with those entering it from among the disbelievers."

The use of the passive form "it was said" (قيل) and the general expression "those who enter" (الداخلين) indicates the insignificance of their affair and their total lack of honor; no concern was shown for them regardless of where they perished.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا امْرَأَتَ فِرْعَوْنَ إِذْ قالَتْ رَبِّ ابْنِ لِي عِنْدَكَ بَيْتاً فِي الْجَنَّةِ** **"And Allah draws an example for those who believe: the wife of Pharaoh, when she said, ‘My Lord! Build me a home near You in paradise’"** (66:11). The discussion regarding His saying **لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا** **"for those who believe"** is the same as the discussion regarding **لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا** **"for those who disbelieve."**

Regarding His saying: **إِذْ قالَتْ رَبِّ ابْنِ لِي عِنْدَكَ بَيْتاً فِي الْجَنَّةِ** **"when she said, ‘My Lord! Build me a home near You in paradise’"**, the Glorified has summarized everything she sought in her life and aimed for in her journey of servitude (العبودية) within this single request she made to her Lord. This is because when faith (الإيمان) reaches perfection, the outward and inward become unified, and the heart and tongue harmonize. Thus, a person says only what they do and does only what they say; what they hope for, long for, or ask with their tongue is exactly what they intend through their actions.

Since Allah, in illustrating her condition and pointing to her special station in servitude (العبودية), recounted a prayer she made, it indicates that this prayer was a comprehensive title for her servitude and the path she walked throughout her life. Her request entails that Allah build for her a house "near Him" (عنده) in Paradise, deliver her from Pharaoh and his work, and deliver her from the wrongdoing people. She chose the proximity of her Lord and nearness to Him over being the companion and beloved of Pharaoh while being the Queen of Egypt. She preferred a house built for her by her Lord over the house of Pharaoh, which contained everything the souls desire and hearts long for, beyond which hopes usually stop. Her soul had turned away from the adornment of the worldly life (الحياة الدنيا) even while it was at her feet, and she attached herself to the honor and proximity (الزلفى) found with her Lord. Thus, she believed in the Unseen (الغيب) and remained steadfast in her faith until she passed away.

This firm footing is what brought her forward to the point where Allah made her an example for those who believe. He summarized her state and what she sought and worked for throughout her life in the path of servitude in a request He narrated from her. Its meaning is nothing other than that she detached herself from everything that distracted her from her Lord and took refuge with her Lord, seeking proximity to Him, the Exalted, and residence in the Abode of His Honor.

Thus, His saying: **امْرَأَتَ فِرْعَوْنَ** **"the wife of Pharaoh"** refers to her, and her name, according to the reports, was Asiyah. His saying: **إِذْ قالَتْ رَبِّ ابْنِ لِي عِنْدَكَ بَيْتاً فِي الْجَنَّةِ** **"when she said, ‘My Lord! Build me a home near You in paradise’"**—the combination of the house being built "near Allah" and "in Paradise" is because Paradise is the Abode of Proximity to Allah and the neighborhood of the Lord of the worlds, as He, the Exalted, said: **بَلْ أَحْياءٌ عِنْدَ رَبِّهِمْ يُرْزَقُونَ** **"rather they are alive with their Lord, provided for"** (3:169).

Furthermore, presence with Him, the Exalted, and proximity to Him is a spiritual honor (كرامة معنوية), while settling in Paradise is a formal honor (كرامة صورية); asking for both is a request for the combination of both honors.

His saying: **وَنَجِّنِي مِنْ فِرْعَوْنَ وَعَمَلِهِ** **"and deliver me from Pharaoh and his conduct"** (66:11) is a disavowal (تبر) of him and a request that Allah deliver her from the person of Pharaoh and from his conduct, which the necessity of companionship and cohabitation would otherwise invite her to share in or be tainted by. It has been said that "conduct" (العمل) here refers to sexual intercourse.

His saying: **وَنَجِّنِي مِنَ الْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ** **"and deliver me from the wrongdoing people"** (66:11)—referring to the people of Pharaoh—is another disavowal and a request that Allah deliver her from the general society, just as the previous phrase was a request to be delivered from the private society.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَمَرْيَمَ ابْنَتَ عِمْرانَ الَّتِي أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَها فَنَفَخْنا فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِنا** **"And Mary, daughter of ‘Imran, who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her of Our Spirit"** (66:12) is coordinated with "the wife of Pharaoh," and the implied meaning is: "And Allah draws an example for those who believe: Mary..."

Allah set her as an example by her name and praised her; He did not mention any woman by name in His speech other than her. Her name is mentioned in the Qur'an in over thirty places across more than twenty Surahs.

His saying: **الَّتِي أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَها فَنَفَخْنا فِيهِ مِنْ رُوحِنا** **"who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her of Our Spirit"** is a praise of her for her modesty. This mention is repeated in the Qur'an, perhaps in response to the slander fabricated against her by the Jews, as He, the Exalted, said: **وَقَوْلِهِمْ عَلى مَرْيَمَ بُهْتاناً عَظِيماً** **"and for their speaking against Mary a monstrous calumny"** (4:156). In Surah al-Anbiya, in a similar account, it says: **وَالَّتِي أَحْصَنَتْ فَرْجَها فَنَفَخْنا فِيها** **"And her who guarded her chastity, so We breathed into her"** (21:91).

His saying: **وَصَدَّقَتْ بِكَلِماتِ رَبِّها** **"and she confirmed the words of her Lord"** (66:12) means, as has been said, that which Allah, the Glorified, spoke of revelation to His Prophets. It has also been said that it refers to His promise, His threat, His command, and His prohibition; however, this would make the subsequent mention of "His Books" redundant.

His saying: **وَكُتُبِهِ** **"and His Books"** (66:12) refers to those containing the divine laws (شرائع) sent down from heaven, such as the Torah and the Gospel, as is the terminology of the Qur'an. Perhaps the meaning of her confirming the words of her Lord and His Books is her being a "truthful one" (صديقة), as in His saying, the Exalted: **مَا الْمَسِيحُ ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ إِلَّا رَسُولٌ قَدْ خَلَتْ مِنْ قَبْلِهِ الرُّسُلُ وَأُمُّهُ صِدِّيقَةٌ** **"The Messiah, son of Mary, is but an apostle. Certainly [other] apostles have passed before him, and his mother was a truthful woman"** (5:75).

His saying: **وَكانَتْ مِنَ الْقانِتِينَ** **"and she was one of the obedient"** (66:12) means she was among the people who are obedient to Allah, humble before Him, and constant in this state. The masculine plural is used here to encompass both genders.

This meaning is supported by the fact that "devout obedience" (القنوت) in this sense occurs in what Allah recounted of the angels' call to her: **يا مَرْيَمُ اقْنُتِي لِرَبِّكِ وَاسْجُدِي وَارْكَعِي مَعَ الرَّاكِعِينَ** **"O Mary, be obedient to your Lord and prostrate and bow down with those who bow [in prayer]"** (3:43). It has also been said that "the obedient" (القانتين) could refer to her kinsfolk and tribe from whom Mary came, as they were a household of righteousness and obedience; however, this is far-fetched given what was previously mentioned.

Furthermore, what is appropriate for the parable being an allusion to the two wives of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) is that "the obedient" (القانتين) refers to all people of obedience and humility toward Allah, the Exalted.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *Tafsir al-Burhan*, from Sharaf al-Din al-Najafi, reaching back to Abu ‘Abdillah (peace be upon him), that he said regarding His saying, the Exalted: **ضَرَبَ اللهُ مَثَلاً لِلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا ـ امْرَأَتَ نُوحٍ وَامْرَأَتَ لُوطٍ** — "This is a parable Allah struck for ‘A’ishah and Hafsah, as they conspired against the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and disclosed his secret."

In *al-Majma‘*: From Abu Musa, from the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), who said: "Many men reached perfection, but none among women reached perfection except four: Asiyah daughter of Muzahim, the wife of Pharaoh; Maryam daughter of ‘Imran; Khadijah daughter of Khuwaylid; and Fatimah daughter of Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم)."

In *al-Durr al-Manthur*: Ahmad, al-Tabarani, and al-Hakim (who authenticated it) narrated from Ibn ‘Abbas that he said: "The Messenger of Allah said: 'The best of the women of the people of Paradise are Khadijah daughter of Khuwaylid, Fatimah daughter of Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم), Maryam daughter of ‘Imran, and Asiyah daughter of Muzahim, the wife of Pharaoh—along with what Allah has recounted to us of their story in the Qur'an: **قالَتْ رَبِّ ابْنِ لِي عِنْدَكَ بَيْتاً فِي الْجَنَّةِ** (she said, ‘My Lord! Build me a home near You in paradise’)."

In the same source, al-Tabarani narrated from Sa‘d ibn Junadah that he said: "The Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) said: 'Indeed, Allah has married me in Paradise to Maryam daughter of ‘Imran, the wife of Pharaoh, and the sister of Moses.'"

**I say:** The wife of Pharaoh, according to what has been mentioned in the narrations, was killed; her husband Pharaoh killed her when he discovered that she believed in Allah alone. The narrations differ regarding the manner of her killing.

In some of them, it is stated that when he discovered her faith, he demanded she return to disbelief, but she refused anything but faith. So he ordered a massive boulder to be thrown upon her so she would be crushed under it, and that was done to her.

In others, it is said that when she was brought for punishment, she prayed with what Allah recounted from her in His speech: **رَبِّ ابْنِ لِي عِنْدَكَ بَيْتاً فِي الْجَنَّةِ** — "My Lord! Build me a home near You in paradise," and so Allah answered her. She saw her home in Paradise, her soul was taken from her, and the boulder was cast upon a body in which there was no soul.

In yet others, it is said that Pharaoh pinned her down with four stakes, laid her on her chest, placed a millstone on her back, and exposed her to the heat of the sun. And Allah knows best.
